free_text: Structure of 12N using 11C+p resonance scattering
Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's
(LBNL) 88-inch cyclotron.
K500 superconducting cyclotron
Berkeley: 10-MeV proton cyclotron was used as a driver
accelerator, producing 11C, which was then transported
as CO2 and injected into the Advanced ECR ion source
(AECR-U)
TAM: a 11C secondary beam,with a beam purity of 99.9%.
Berkeley: The target ladder held a 12.4-mg/cm2-thick
nickel degrader followed by a 18.4-mg/cm2-thick (CH2)n
(= polyethylene) foil.
TAM: a scattering chamber filled with methane (CH4) gas
that was placed at the MARS focal plane
A parallel plate avalanche counter (PPAC) was
placed in the front of the entrance to this scattering
chamber at T & AM.
A DE (thickness 72 microm)-E (thickness 3 mm)
Si-detector telescope was mounted on one scattering
chamber arm, about 14.6 cm away from the target at
Berkeley
Thick target inverse kinematics (TTIK) method at
Berkeley
DR
No information
For 5,10 degr. data taken from Figs. 3 to 5.
For 0 and 15 degree, data received from
K. Perajarvi on 2/27/2007

Note that in the case of the gas target data,
the actual detection angle changes as a function
of the 11C beam energy.
